5 David answered, "Yes, we have stayed away from women, as usual on my trips. The bodies of my young men are holy, even on normal jobs. So they are even more holy now."
6 So the priest gave him the holy bread. There was no other bread there but the Bread of the Presence. It had been taken from before the LORD. They had put fresh hot bread in its place.
7 One of Saul's servants was there that day. The LORD held him back there. His name was Doeg the Edomite. He was Saul's head shepherd.
8 Then David asked Ahimelech, "Do you have a spear or sword here? I did not bring my sword or weapons. The king's job was very urgent."

9 The priest replied, "The sword of Goliath the Philistine is here. You killed him in the Valley of Elah. It is wrapped in a cloth behind the ephod. You may take it if you want. There is no other sword here." David said, "There is no sword like it. Give it to me."
10 That day David ran from Saul. He went to Achish, the king of Gath.
11 But the servants of Achish said, "Is this not David, the king of the land? Did they not sing about him in their dances? They said, 'Saul has killed thousands, and David tens of thousands.'"
12 David thought hard about these words. He became very afraid of Achish, the king of Gath.
13 So he changed how he acted in front of them. He acted crazy in their hands. He made marks on the gate doors. He let spit run down his beard.
14 Then Achish said to his servants, "Look, the man is crazy! Why have you brought him to me?
15 Do I need crazy men? Why did you bring this man to shout like a mad man here? Must this man come into my house?"

title: 1 Samuel 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 So David left Gath and hid in the cave of Adullam. His brothers and his father's family heard about it. They went down to him there.
2 All who were in trouble or in debt or angry gathered around him. He became their leader. About four hundred men were with him.
3 From there David went to Mizpeh of Moab. He said to the king of Moab, "Please let my father and mother stay with you. Let them stay until I know what God will do for me."
4 So he left them with the king of Moab. They stayed with him the whole time David was in the safe place.
5 Then the prophet Gad said to David, "Do not stay in the safe place. Leave and go into the land of Judah." So David left and went to the forest of Hereth.

6 Soon Saul heard that someone had found David and his men. At that time Saul was in Gibeah. He sat under the tamarisk tree on the hill at Gibeah. His spear was in his hand. All his servants stood around him.
7 Then Saul said to his servants, "Listen, men of Benjamin! Will the son of Jesse give all of you fields and vineyards? Will he make you leaders of thousands or hundreds?

8 Is that why all of you have plotted against me? Not one of you told me about my own son. He made a promise with the son of Jesse. Not one of you cares about me. Not one of you told me my son turned my servant against me. My servant waits to attack me, as he does today."
9 But Doeg the Edomite stood with Saul's servants. He answered, "I saw the son of Jesse come to Nob. He came to Ahimelech son of Ahitub.
10 Ahimelech asked the LORD for him and gave him food. He also gave him the sword of Goliath the Philistine."
11 Then the king sent messengers to call Ahimelech the priest. He also called the son of Ahitub and his father's whole family. They were priests at Nob. All of them came to the king.
12 "Listen now, son of Ahitub," said Saul. "Here I am, my lord," he replied.

13 Saul asked him, "Why have you and the son of Jesse plotted against me? You gave him bread and a sword. You asked God for him. Now he can rise up against me. He waits to attack me, as he does today."
14 Ahimelech answered the king, "Who is as faithful as David among all your servants? He is the king's son by marriage. He is the leader of your guards. He is honored in your house.
15 Was that day the first time I asked God for him? Never! Let the king not blame me or my father's family. I knew nothing about this whole matter — not in part or in whole."
16 But the king replied, "You will surely die, Ahimelech! You and all your father's house will die!"

17 Then the king ordered the guards at his side. "Turn and kill the priests of the LORD! They too helped David. They knew he was running away. But they did not tell me." But the king's servants would not raise a hand to hit the priests of the LORD.
18 So the king ordered Doeg, "You turn and strike down the priests!" And Doeg the Edomite turned and struck down the priests himself. That day he killed eighty-five priests who wore the linen robe.
19 He also killed the people of Nob, the city of the priests. He killed its men and women, children and babies. He killed its cattle, donkeys, and sheep with the sword.
20 But one son of Ahimelech son of Ahitub escaped. His name was Abiathar. He fled to David.
21 Abiathar told David that Saul had killed the priests of the LORD.
22 Then David said to Abiathar, "I knew that Doeg the Edomite was there that day. I knew he would surely tell Saul. I am guilty for the deaths of everyone in your father's house.
23 Stay with me. Do not be afraid. The one who wants your life also wants mine. You will be safe with me."

title: 1 Samuel 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 People told David, "Look, the Philistines are fighting against Keilah. They are stealing grain from the threshing floors."
2 So David asked the LORD, "Should I go and attack these Philistines?" The LORD said to David, "Go, attack the Philistines, and save Keilah."
3 But David's men said to him, "Look, we are afraid here in Judah. We will be much more afraid if we go to Keilah against the Philistine armies."
4 So David asked the LORD again. The LORD answered him, "Go at once to Keilah. I will give the Philistines to you."
5 Then David and his men went to Keilah. They fought against the Philistines and took their farm animals. They struck them with a strong blow. So David saved the people of Keilah.
6 (Now Abiathar, the son of Ahimelech, had brought the ephod with him when he ran to David at Keilah.)
7 People told Saul that David had gone to Keilah. Saul said, "God has given him to me. He has trapped himself by going into a town with gates and bars."
8 Then Saul called all his troops to go to war at Keilah. He wanted to trap David and his men there.
9 David learned that Saul was planning evil against him. He said to Abiathar the priest, "Bring the ephod."
10 Then David said, "O LORD, God of Israel, your servant has heard this. Saul plans to come to Keilah and destroy the city because of me.
11 Will the people of Keilah hand me over to him? Will Saul come down, as your servant has heard? O LORD, God of Israel, please tell your servant." The LORD said, "He will."
12 So David asked, "Will the people of Keilah hand me and my men over to Saul?" The LORD said, "They will."
13 Then David and his men, about six hundred strong, left Keilah. They moved from place to place. People told Saul that David had escaped from Keilah. So Saul did not go out.
14 David stayed in safe places in the wilderness. He stayed in the hill country of the Wilderness of Ziph. Day after day Saul searched for him. But God did not give David to him.
15 While David was in Horesh in the Wilderness of Ziph, he saw that Saul had come out to kill him.
16 Then Jonathan, Saul's son, came to David in Horesh. He helped David trust God more.
17 Jonathan said, "Do not be afraid. My father Saul will never lay a hand on you. You will be king over Israel. I will be next to you in power. Even my father Saul knows this is true."
18 So the two of them made a promise before the LORD. David stayed in Horesh, and Jonathan went home.
19 Then the Ziphites came up to Saul at Gibeah. They said, "David is hiding among us, isn't he? He is in the safe places at Horesh, on the hill of Hachilah south of Jeshimon.
20 Now, O king, come down whenever you want. We will hand him over to you."
21 Saul replied, "May the LORD bless you, for you have shown me kindness.
22 Please go and find out more. Watch carefully where he goes and who has seen him there. People tell me he is very clever and tricky.
23 Find out all the places where he hides. Then come back to me with sure news. I will go with you. If he is in the land, I will search for him among all the family groups of Judah."
24 So they left and went to Ziph ahead of Saul. Now David and his men were in the Wilderness of Maon. They were in the Arabah, south of Jeshimon.
25 Saul and his men went to look for him. When David was told about it, he went down to the rock. He stayed in the Wilderness of Maon. When Saul heard this, he chased David there.
26 Saul was going along one side of the mountain. David and his men were on the other side. David hurried to get away. But Saul and his men were closing in to catch them.
27 Then a messenger came to Saul. He said, "Come quickly! The Philistines have attacked the land!"
28 So Saul stopped chasing David and went to meet the Philistines. That is why people call that place Sela-hammahlekoth (Rock of Parting).
29 Then David went up from there and lived in the safe places of En-gedi.

title: 1 Samuel 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 When Saul came back from chasing the Philistines, someone told him, "David is in the desert of En-gedi."
2 So Saul took three thousand chosen men from all Israel. He went to look for David near the Rocks of the Wild Goats.
3 Saul came to the sheep pens along the road. There was a cave there, and he went in to use the toilet. David and his men were hiding in the back of the cave.

4 David's men said to him, "This is the day the LORD spoke about. He said, 'I will give your enemy to you. You may do what you wish with him.'" Then David quietly moved close and cut off the edge of Saul's robe.
5 After that, David felt guilty in his heart. He had cut off the edge of Saul's robe.
6 So he said to his men, "**The LORD forbid that I should do such a thing to my master, the LORD's anointed.** I will never lift my hand against him. He is the LORD's anointed."
7 With these words David stopped his men. He did not let them attack Saul. Then Saul left the cave and went on his way.
8 After that, David got up and went out of the cave. He called out to Saul, "My lord the king!" Saul looked behind him. David bowed low to the ground.
9 David said to Saul, "Why do you listen to men who say, 'Look, David wants to hurt you'?

10 Today you have seen with your own eyes. The LORD gave you to me in the cave. Some told me to kill you, but I let you live. I said, 'I will not lift my hand against my lord. He is the LORD's anointed.'

11 Look, my father, see the edge of your robe in my hand. I cut it off, but I did not kill you. See and know there is no evil or wrong in my hands. I have not sinned against you. Yet you are chasing me to take my life.
12 May the LORD judge between you and me. May the LORD pay you back, but my hand will never harm you.
13 The old saying tells us, 'Evil comes from evil people.' But my hand will never harm you.
14 Who has the king of Israel come out against? Who are you chasing? A dead dog? A flea?
15 May the LORD be our judge between you and me. May He see my case, speak for me, and save me from your hand."
16 When David finished speaking, Saul called back, "Is that your voice, David my son?" Then Saul cried out loud.
17 Saul said to David, "You are more right than I am. You paid me back with good, but I paid you back with evil.
18 Today you have shown how well you treated me. The LORD gave me to you, but you did not kill me.
19 When a man finds his enemy, does he let him go safely? May the LORD reward you with good for what you did today.
20 Now I know for sure that you will be king. The kingdom of Israel will be strong in your hands.
21 So now, promise me before the LORD. You will not kill my children. You will not erase my family name."
22 So David gave his promise to Saul. Then Saul returned home. But David and his men went up to the hideout.

title: 1 Samuel 25
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 Samuel died. All Israel came together to cry for him. They buried him at his home in Ramah. Then David went down to the desert of Paran.
2 A man lived in Maon. His business was in Carmel. He was very rich. He had a thousand goats and three thousand sheep. He was cutting the wool from his sheep in Carmel.
3 His name was Nabal. His wife's name was Abigail. She was a smart and beautiful woman. But her husband was mean. He came from the family of Caleb. He did bad things to people.
4 David was in the desert. He heard that Nabal was cutting wool from his sheep.
5 So David sent ten young men. He told them, "Go up to Nabal in Carmel. Say hello from me."
6 "Tell him, 'May you have long life. May peace come to you, your house, and all you have.'"
7 "I hear it is time for cutting wool. Your shepherds were with us. We did not hurt them. They lost nothing while they were in Carmel."
8 "Ask your young men. They will tell you. Please be kind to my young men. We come on a feast day. Please give what you can to your servants and to your son David."
9 David's young men went to Nabal. They said all these words from David. Then they waited.
10 But Nabal said to them, "Who is David? Who is this son of Jesse? Many servants run away from their owners these days."
11 "Why should I take my bread, my water, and my meat? I killed it for my workers. Should I give it to men I do not know?"
12 So David's men turned around. They went back. They told David all these words.
13 David said to his men, "Put on your swords!" So they all put on their swords. About four hundred men went with David. Two hundred men stayed with the supplies.
14 One of Nabal's young men spoke to Abigail, Nabal's wife. "Look, David sent men from the desert to greet our master. But he shouted at them."
15 "But these men were very good to us. We were in the field. They did not hurt us. We lost nothing while we lived near them."
16 "They were like a wall that kept us safe by day and night. We were with our sheep near them all the time."
17 "Think carefully about what to do. Trouble is coming to our master and all his house. He is such a bad man that no one can talk to him!"

18 Then Abigail hurried. She took two hundred loaves of bread, two skins of wine, and five cooked sheep. She also took five large bowls of cooked grain, a hundred bunches of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs. She put them on donkeys.
19 She said to her young men, "Go ahead of me. I will come after you." But she did not tell her husband Nabal.
20 Abigail rode her donkey into a narrow valley in the hills. She saw David and his men coming down to meet her.
21 David had just said, "I protected all that this man had in the desert. He lost nothing of his. But he paid me back bad for good."
22 "May God punish me very hard if I let even one of Nabal's men live until morning."
23 When Abigail saw David, she quickly got off the donkey. She bowed her face to the ground before him.
24 She fell at his feet and said, "My lord, blame only me. Please let your servant speak to you. Hear what your servant says."
25 "My lord, do not pay attention to that bad man Nabal. His name fits him. His name means Fool. Foolish things follow him. I, your servant, did not see your young men."
26 "My lord, as the LORD truly lives, and as you live: the LORD has kept you from killing. He kept you from paying back wrong by your own hand. May your enemies be like Nabal."
27 "Now let this gift from your servant be given to the young men who follow my lord."
28 "Please forgive my wrong, your servant. The LORD will surely make a strong family line for my lord. You fight the LORD's battles. May no evil be in you all your life."
29 "Someone may chase you and try to kill you. But the LORD your God will keep my lord safe in his care of life. He will throw the lives of your enemies away, like stones from a sling."
30 "The LORD will do for my lord all the good he promised. He will make you ruler over Israel."

31 "Then my lord will feel no regret or guilt in your heart. You will not have killed without need. You will not have paid back wrong yourself. When the LORD is good to my lord, please remember your servant."
32 Then David said to Abigail, "Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel. He sent you to meet me today!"
33 "Blessed is your good sense. And blessed are you. Today you kept me from killing. You kept me from paying back wrong by my own hand."
34 "As the LORD lives, the God of Israel, who held me back from hurting you: if you had not come quickly to meet me, no man of Nabal's would still be alive by morning."
35 Then David took from her hand what she had brought him. He said to her, "Go home in peace. See, I have listened to your words. I grant what you ask."
36 Abigail went back to Nabal. He was in the house, holding a feast like a king. He was very happy and very drunk. So she told him nothing until morning.
37 In the morning, Nabal was not drunk anymore. His wife told him about these things. His heart stopped inside him. He became like a stone.
38 About ten days later, the LORD struck Nabal. He died.

39 David heard that Nabal was dead. He said, "Blessed be the LORD. He stood up for me against Nabal's insult. He kept his servant from doing evil. The LORD has put Nabal's evil on his own head." Then David sent word to Abigail. He asked her to marry him.
40 David's servants came to Abigail at Carmel. They said, "David sent us to take you to be his wife."
41 She stood up, then bowed her face to the ground. She said, "Here is your servant. I am ready to serve. I will even wash the feet of my lord's servants."
42 So Abigail hurried. She got on a donkey. Five of her young female servants went with her. She followed David's men and became his wife.
43 David had also married Ahinoam of Jezreel. So both she and Abigail were his wives.
44 But Saul had given his daughter Michal to Palti, son of Laish, from Gallim. Michal had been David's wife.

title: 1 Samuel 26
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 Then the Ziphites came to Saul at Gibeah. They said, "David is hiding on the hill of Hachilah, across from Jeshimon."
2 So Saul took three thousand chosen men of Israel with him. They went down to the Desert of Ziph to look for David there.
3 Saul set up camp beside the road on the hill of Hachilah, across from Jeshimon. But David was living in the desert. He learned that Saul had followed him there.
4 So David sent out spies. They made sure that Saul had come.

5 Then David went to the place where Saul had camped. He saw where Saul and Abner son of Ner were lying down. Abner was the leader of his army. Saul was lying in the middle of the camp. The troops were camped around him.
6 Then David asked Ahimelech the Hittite and Abishai son of Zeruiah. Abishai was Joab's brother. David said, "Who will go down with me to Saul in the camp?" Abishai answered, "I will go with you."
7 That night David and Abishai came to the troops. Saul was lying there asleep in the middle of the camp. His spear was stuck in the ground by his head. Abner and the troops were lying around him.
8 Abishai said to David, "Today God has given your enemy into your hand. Now please let me push the spear through him into the ground. One blow is enough. I will not need to strike him twice!"
9 But David said to Abishai, "Do not kill him. Who can lift a hand against the LORD's anointed king and not be guilty?"
10 David added, "The LORD is alive, and I am sure of this. The LORD himself will strike him down. His day will come and he will die. Or he will go into battle and die there.
11 But may the LORD stop me from lifting my hand against his anointed king. Instead, take the spear and water jug by his head. Then let us go."
12 So David took the spear and water jug by Saul's head. Then they left. No one saw them. No one knew. No one woke up. They all stayed asleep. A deep sleep from the LORD had fallen on them.
13 Then David crossed to the other side. He stood on top of the mountain far away. There was a wide space between them.
14 David shouted to the troops and to Abner son of Ner. He said, "Will you not answer me, Abner?" Abner replied, "Who is calling to the king?"
15 David said to Abner, "You are a man, aren't you? Who in Israel is equal to you? Why did you not protect your master the king? Someone came to kill him.

16 What you have done is not good. The LORD is alive, and I am sure. All of you deserve to die. You did not protect your master, the LORD's anointed king. Now look around. Where are the king's spear and water jug? They were by his head."
17 Then Saul knew David's voice. He asked, "Is that your voice, David my son?" David said, "It is my voice, my master and king."
18 He went on, "Why is my master chasing his servant? What have I done? What evil is in my hand?

19 Now please, may my master the king hear his servant's words. If the LORD has turned you against me, may he accept a gift. But if men have done it, may they be cursed before the LORD! Today they have driven me away from the LORD's land. They say, 'Go, serve other gods.'
20 So do not let my blood fall to the ground far from the LORD. The king of Israel has come out to look for a flea. He hunts me like one who hunts a bird in the mountains."
21 Then Saul replied, "I have sinned. Come back, David my son. I will never harm you again. Today you saw my life as precious. I have acted like a fool. I have made a big mistake!"
22 David answered, "Here is the king's spear. Let one of the young men come over and get it.
23 May the LORD pay back every man for doing right and being faithful. The LORD gave you into my hand today. But I would not lift my hand against his anointed king.
24 I valued your life today. So may the LORD value my life. May he save me from all trouble."
25 Saul said to him, "May you be blessed, David my son. You will do great things. You will surely win." So David went on his way. And Saul returned home.

title: 1 Samuel 27
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 But David thought to himself, "One day Saul will kill me. The best thing is to run to the Philistines' land. Then Saul will stop looking for me in Israel. I will escape from him."
2 So David went out with his six hundred men. They went to Achish son of Maoch, the king of Gath.
3 David and his men lived in Gath with Achish. Each man had his family with him. David had his two wives: Ahinoam of Jezreel, and Abigail of Carmel, Nabal's widow.
4 Saul heard that David had run to Gath. So he stopped looking for him.

5 Then David said to Achish, "If you are pleased with me, please give me a place in one of the small country towns. Then I can live there. Why should I, your servant, live in the king's city with you?"
6 That day Achish gave him Ziklag. Even today it still belongs to the kings of Judah.
7 David lived in the Philistine land for one year and four months.
8 Now David and his men went up and attacked the Geshurites, the Girzites, and the Amalekites. (From old times these people had lived in the land that reached to Shur and Egypt.)
9 When David attacked a place, he killed every man and woman. He took the sheep, cattle, donkeys, camels, and clothes. Then he went back to Achish.
10 Achish would ask him, "Where did you attack today?" David would answer, "The Negev of Judah," or "The Negev of Jerahmeel," or "The Negev of the Kenites."

11 David did not let any man or woman live. He did not want them brought to Gath. He said, "If not, they will tell on us. They will say, 'This is what David did.'" David did this the whole time he lived in the Philistine land.
12 So Achish believed David. He thought, "David is now so hated by his own people Israel. He will always serve me."

title: 1 Samuel 28
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 In those days the Philistines gathered their army to fight Israel. Achish said to David, "You must know that you and your men will go to battle with me."
2 David replied, "Then you will see what your servant can do." Achish said, "Very well. I will make you my guard forever."
3 By this time Samuel had died. All Israel had cried for him. They had buried him in Ramah, his own city. Saul had sent away all the people who talk with spirits from the land.
4 The Philistines came together and camped at Shunem. Saul gathered all Israel and camped at Gilboa.
5 When Saul saw the Philistine army, he was afraid. He shook with great fear.
6 He asked the LORD, but the LORD did not answer him. No answer came by dreams or Urim or prophets.
7 Then Saul said to his servants, "Find me a woman who talks with spirits. I will go and ask her." His servants said, "There is one at Endor."
8 So Saul dressed in other clothes to hide who he was. He went out with two of his men. They came to the woman at night. Saul said, "Call up a spirit for me. Bring up the one I name."
9 But the woman replied, "You know what Saul has done. He has killed the people who talk with spirits in the land. Why do you try to get me killed?"
10 Then Saul made a promise to her by the LORD. He said, "As surely as the LORD lives, no punishment will come on you for this."
11 "Who shall I bring up for you?" the woman asked. "Bring up Samuel," he replied.
12 But when the woman saw Samuel, she cried out loudly. She said to Saul, "Why have you tricked me? You are Saul!"
13 "Do not be afraid," the king replied. "What do you see?" "I see a god coming up out of the earth," she answered.
14 "What does he look like?" Saul asked. "An old man is coming up," she replied. "He is wearing a robe." So Saul knew it was Samuel. He bowed his face to the ground.

15 Then Samuel said to Saul, "Why have you disturbed me by bringing me up?" Saul replied, "I am in deep trouble. The Philistines are fighting against me. God has turned away from me. He no longer answers me by prophets or by dreams. So I have called you to tell me what to do."
16 Samuel asked, "Why do you ask me? The LORD has turned away from you. He has become your enemy.
17 The LORD has done what He said through me. He has taken the kingdom out of your hand. He has given it to your neighbor David.
18 You did not obey the LORD. You did not carry out His strong anger against Amalek. So the LORD has done this to you today.
19 Also, the LORD will hand Israel over to the Philistines with you. Tomorrow you and your sons will be with me. The LORD will hand the army of Israel over to the Philistines."
20 At once Saul fell flat on the ground. He was filled with fear by the words of Samuel. His strength was gone, because he had not eaten all that day and night.
21 The woman came to Saul and saw how shaken he was. She said to him, "Look, your servant has obeyed you. I put my own life in danger and did what you said.
22 Now please listen to your servant. Let me set a small piece of bread before you. Then you may eat and have strength to go on your way."
23 Saul refused, saying, "I will not eat." But his servants joined the woman in begging him. So he listened to them. He got up from the ground and sat on the bed.
24 The woman had a young fat cow at her house. She quickly killed it. She also took flour, mixed it, and baked bread without yeast.
25 She served it to Saul and his servants, and they ate. Then that night they got up and left.

title: 1 Samuel 29
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 Now the Philistines gathered all their armies at Aphek. Israel set up camp by the spring in Jezreel.
2 The Philistine leaders led out their soldiers in groups of hundreds and thousands. David and his men marched behind them with Achish.

3 Then the Philistine leaders asked, "Why are these Hebrews here?" Achish replied, "Is this not David, a servant of King Saul of Israel? He has been with me for many days, even years. From the day he left Saul until today, I have found nothing wrong with him."

4 But the Philistine leaders were angry with Achish. They told him, "Send that man back. Let him return to the place you gave him. He must not go down with us into battle. He may become our enemy during the war. What better way for him to win back his master's trust than with the heads of our soldiers?
5 Is this not the David they sing about in their dances? They sing, 'Saul has killed thousands, and David has killed tens of thousands.'"

6 So Achish called David and told him, "The LORD is alive, so I promise: you have been honest in my eyes. It seems right that you should go with me in the army. From the day you came to me until this day, I have found nothing wrong with you. But the leaders do not approve of you.
7 So turn back now and leave in peace. Do not do anything to upset the Philistine leaders."
8 "But what have I done?" David replied. "What have you found wrong with your servant? From the day I came to you until today, why can I not go and fight the enemies of my lord the king?"
9 Achish replied, "I know that you are as good in my eyes as an angel of God. But the Philistine leaders have said, 'He must not go into battle with us.'
10 Now then, get up early in the morning. Take the servants of your master who came with you. Go as soon as it is light."
11 So David and his men got up early in the morning. They returned to the land of the Philistines. And the Philistines went up to Jezreel.

title: 1 Samuel 30
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 On the third day, David and his men came back to Ziklag. The Amalekites had attacked the Negev and Ziklag. They had burned the town down.
2 They had taken the women and all the people as prisoners. They took both the young and the old. They had not killed anyone, but they took them away.
3 When David and his men came to the town, they found it burned down. Their wives and sons and daughters were taken as prisoners.
4 So David and his men cried out loud. They wept until they had no strength left to weep.
5 David's two wives had been taken as prisoners too. They were Ahinoam of Jezreel and Abigail the widow of Nabal of Carmel.
6 David was very upset. The people talked of killing him with stones. Every man was sad in his heart for his sons and daughters. But **David found strength in the LORD his God**.
7 Then David said to Abiathar the priest, the son of Ahimelech, "Bring me the ephod." So Abiathar brought it to him.
8 David asked the LORD, "Should I chase these attackers? Will I catch them?" The LORD answered, "Chase them. You will catch them and save the prisoners."
9 So David and his six hundred men went to the Brook of Besor. Some men stayed behind there.
10 Two hundred men were too tired to cross the brook. But David and four hundred men kept on chasing.
11 His men found an Egyptian man in the field. They brought him to David. They gave him water to drink and food to eat.
12 They gave him a piece of fig cake and two bunches of raisins. He ate and got his strength back. He had not eaten or drunk for three days and three nights.
13 Then David asked him, "Whose servant are you? Where do you come from?" He said, "I am an Egyptian, the slave of an Amalekite. My master left me three days ago when I got sick.
14 We attacked the Negev of the Cherethites, the land of Judah, and the Negev of Caleb. We also burned down Ziklag."
15 David asked, "Will you lead me to these attackers?" The man said, "Promise me by God that you will not kill me. Do not give me back to my master. Then I will lead you to them."
16 So he led David down to them. The Amalekites were spread out over all the land. They were eating, drinking, and celebrating. They had taken many things from the Philistines and from Judah.
17 David fought them from the evening until the next evening. Not one man got away, except four hundred young men. They ran away on camels.
18 So David got back everything the Amalekites had taken. He got back his two wives too.
19 Nothing was missing — young or old, son or daughter, or any of the things they had taken. David brought everything back.
20 He also got back all the sheep and cattle. His men drove them ahead and called out, "This is David's plunder!"
21 David came back to the two hundred men who had been too tired to follow him from the Brook of Besor. They came out to meet David and his men. As David came near, he greeted them.
22 But the bad and worthless men among David's troops said, "These men did not go with us. So we will not share what we got back with them. They can only take their own wives and children and go."
23 But David said, "My brothers, you must not do this with what the LORD has given us. He kept us safe. He gave the attackers into our hands.
24 Who will listen to what you say? The man who went to fight and the man who stayed with the supplies will get the same share. They will share alike."
25 And it has been like this from that day on. David made this a rule for Israel, even to this day.
26 When David came to Ziklag, he sent some of the things to his friends, the elders of Judah. He said, "Here is a gift for you from the things we took from the LORD's enemies."
27 He sent gifts to those in Bethel, Ramoth Negev, and Jattir;
28 to those in Aroer, Siphmoth, and Eshtemoa;
29 to those in Racal, in the cities of the Jerahmeelites, and in the cities of the Kenites;
30 to those in Hormah, Bor-ashan, and Athach;
31 and to those in Hebron and in all the places where David and his men had walked.

title: 1 Samuel 31
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Samuel
paragraphs: 1 The Philistines fought against Israel. The men of Israel ran away from them. Many were killed on Mount Gilboa.
2 The Philistines chased hard after Saul and his sons. They killed Saul's sons Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchishua.
3 The battle grew fierce against Saul. The bowmen caught up with him and hurt him badly.

4 Then Saul said to his armor-bearer, "Pull out your sword and push it through me. If you do not, these foreign men will come and hurt me for fun!" But his armor-bearer was very afraid and refused to do it. So Saul took his own sword and dropped onto it.
5 When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he also dropped onto his own sword and died with him.
6 So Saul, his three sons, his armor-bearer, and all his men died together that same day.

7 The Israelites in the valley and across the Jordan saw what had happened. The army of Israel had run away. Saul and his sons had died. So the people left their cities and ran away. Then the Philistines came and moved into their cities.
8 The next day, the Philistines came to take things from the dead. They found Saul and his three sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.

9 They cut off Saul's head and took off his armor. They sent messengers all through the land of the Philistines. The messengers announced the news in the temples of their false gods and among their people.
10 They put his armor in the temple of the Ashtoreths. They hung his body on the wall of Beth-shan.
11 The people of Jabesh-gilead heard what the Philistines had done to Saul.
12 All their brave men started out and walked all night. They got the bodies of Saul and his sons back from the wall of Beth-shan. When they arrived at Jabesh, they burned the bodies there.
13 Then they took their bones and buried them under the tamarisk tree in Jabesh. They did not eat for seven days.
